verdict.label: RED · Stand down / roll DTE longer
verdict.desc: The U.S. struck Iran overnight and President Trump declared the ceasefire “over,” threatening to “hit them hard” again, with Tehran claiming it targeted 85 U.S. sites in the Gulf — oil jumped and the Dow shed ~577 points (−1.1%). Gap risk is elevated at 66/100 (WARM) and P(≥1% gap) is 44% — above the 35% trigger — so a GREEN verdict is locked out. During an active, open-ended military conflict the overnight gap tail is unquantifiable and skewed down. Stand down on new credit spreads today, or push DTE out past the conflict window; this is not a premium-selling tape.

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Thu Open)
gap.band_note: ABOVE the 35% danger line · GREEN locked out

focus.jbig: War headlines reset the tape · stand down
focus.jtag: U.S. struck Iran overnight, ceasefire declared over — oil spikes, Dow falls hard
focus.jnote: The environment changed overnight — not for the better. The U.S. carried out a series of strikes on Iran after attacks on commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump declared the interim ceasefire “over” at the NATO summit in Ankara and threatened to strike again, and Tehran said it had targeted 85 U.S. military sites in Bahrain and Kuwait. Washington also revoked the waiver that let Iran sell crude. WTI popped ~4.4% to ~$73.5 (Brent ~$78), the VIX rose ~5% to ~16.9, and the Dow dropped ~577 points. Gold slipped ~1.8% toward $4,082 as the dollar firmed and yields rose. This is a live, open-ended conflict — exactly the kind of headline tape a Sniper does not sell premium into.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and Defense (LMT, RTX, NOC) are the groups catching a bid — XLE jumped over 2% as oil supply fear lifts energy and conflict-demand lifts defense. But be honest: these are two-sided war trades, not clean Snipers. One de-escalation headline gaps them straight back down — note Trump himself said he doesn’t think the conflict will restart, pulling the market off its lows. Watch-only, not table-pounders.
focus.notup: Airlines & transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) and other fuel-sensitive cyclicals are pressured by the jet-fuel spike. But selling bear calls into an active-war, VIX-rising tape carries its own violent-squeeze risk on any peace headline — the disciplined answer is to sell nothing new today.
focus.why: Why RED? Composite 66 is deep in WARM and P(≥1% gap) at 44% is well above the 35% trigger — either one alone locks out GREEN. More important than the numbers: an active military exchange with an oil shock is an unscheduled, open-ended tail — worse than a known event like CPI because you cannot time or size around the next headline, and Trump is openly threatening further strikes tonight. Stand down on new credit spreads, or if you must be positioned, push DTE out past the conflict window and cut size hard. Cash is a position today.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.9, up ~4.8% and expanding off the strike headlines
Big events (next 1–3 days) — live war headlines, PEP earnings Thu, DAL Fri; CPI next week
World risk — active U.S.–Iran military exchange, ceasefire declared over, Gulf bases threatened
Volatility trend — front-month VIX rising off lows, curve flattening toward stress
Commodities — oil supply shock, +4–7% on Hormuz risk; the biggest single swing factor

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news and energy was the day’s clearest bid — XLE jumped over 2% with Occidental and Valero leading. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation headline and crude gives back $5–7 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way — and Trump already hinted the conflict may not restart. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a RED day, don’t open new premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above the open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Conflict-demand lifts the defense primes, and structurally the story can run. But the same peace-headline risk cuts the other way: a ceasefire announcement gaps this group down hard. Selling a bull put here is a bet the war continues — not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: Same logic as energy — the bid is a war premium. Stand aside; revisit only once the tape settles.

setups.read: Jet fuel just jumped with crude, a direct headwind for airlines — the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a peace headline squeezes shorts violently, and DAL reports Fri 7/10 (earnings inside any normal DTE). A bear call into a VIX spike with a print pending is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Earnings + war squeeze risk = no. Do not carry an airline spread through Friday’s DAL print.

setups.read: No clean fresh post-earnings gap that qualifies. Penguin Solutions surged on a beat, but it sits inside a risk-off war tape, not a calm one. Q2 season proper hasn’t started — PepsiCo (Thu) and Delta (Fri) are the first big prints. Check back once the calendar fills next week.
setups.check_note: Nothing clean to validate — no qualifying Type A into this tape.

setups.read: Risk-off, war-headline day — the exact tape where breakouts fail. With the Dow down over 500 points and geopolitical risk live, no new clean high-volume ATH breakout is holding into an Iran-strike tape. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline-driven whipsaw
scenarios.sub: Choppy risk-off; the tape lurches on every Iran/oil headline, no clean trend, vol stays bid

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation
scenarios.sub: Iran hits a Gulf base or Hormuz shipping is disrupted; oil runs toward $85–90, equities gap down

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel headline; oil gives back the spike, a relief bounce in stocks, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran retaliation — Tehran named 85 U.S. sites in Bahrain and Kuwait, and Trump threatened to strike again the same night; any actual strike on personnel or bases gaps the whole tape instantly. This is the number-one thing to watch.

Strait of Hormuz shipping — the waterway handles roughly a fifth of the world’s oil, and recent attacks hit a Qatari LNG carrier and a Saudi tanker. Any closure sends crude (and jet fuel) vertical — the week’s biggest tail.
Oil & the 10-year — WTI back near $73–74 feeds inflation fear; the 10Y rose to ~4.50%, a 2-week high, and FOMC minutes flagged upside inflation risk. A rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.
PEP (Thu) & DAL (Fri) — first Q2 prints; Delta is directly exposed to the fuel spike. Single-stock gap risk, not just the index.
CPI & PPI next week — a hot inflation number stacked on top of an oil shock would be the worst-case macro combination, especially with the Fed’s hawkish June-minutes tone.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is just starting — PepsiCo (Thu 7/9) and Delta (Fri 7/10) open it, then the big banks report ~mid-July. With the market already on edge from the Iran shock, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Defensive staple — the market watches it for consumer-demand and pricing signals, and consumer-defensive was one of the only green sectors today. Lower beta than the airlines, but a volume/guidance miss still gaps it. First read of the season.
rows.angle: Don’t hold a PEP spread through Thursday. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A into Friday.

rows.stock_sub: Delta · first airline of the season
rows.why: The Iran-driven fuel spike now cuts against airlines — higher jet fuel pressures margins and guidance, and the EIA sees jet-fuel wholesale prices sharply higher. Directly in the crossfire of this week’s oil move.
rows.angle: Do not carry any airline spread through Friday. A miss-and-gap-down becomes a Type A bear-call setup next Monday.

rows.why: Real season opener — and they land alongside CPI/PPI, stacking macro and single-stock risk. Credit-sensitive names like JPMorgan already sold off ~2–2.5% today. Any bank spread must expire before its print.
rows.angle: Keep any financials spread to 7 days or fewer so it clears the earnings date — and mind the CPI/PPI overlap.

earnings.outro: Wed 7/8 – Fri 7/10: PepsiCo and Delta open the season; the rest is small-cap and off-cycle. It gets real next week: big banks alongside CPI/PPI, then the first mega-caps the week after — all against a live Iran backdrop.

rows.today: Risk-off · Dow −577 pts on the strike
